note: Counting-benchmark bank manifest. GENERATED by benchmarks/bank_manifest_gen.py from benchmarks/bank_families.py (the spec) merged with each family's bank/<id>/provenance.json. Downloaded/converted data lives under benchmarks/bank/ and is GITIGNORED — rerun the fetch_*.py scripts (e.g. on HPC2) to materialize, then rerun this to pin archive hashes + counts. Contract: docs/research/2026-07-13-counting-benchmark-survey.md.

families.title: Model Counting Competition 2023 — tracks 1 (mc) + 2 (wmc)
families.format: DIMACS-CNF (MCC 2021+ headers: `c t mc|wmc`, `c p weight`)

reference.note: MCC reference counts are log10 estimates; compare within a log10 relative tolerance, never exact. Reference values ship with the competition results, not always in the instance archive — bank_check.py looks for a sibling *.log10 / results file and cross-checks small instances with sharpSAT-TD.

families.notes: Reviewer-mandatory. sharpSAT-TD/Ganak/d4 have published numbers on this. Archive holds all 4 tracks; fetch keeps only mc+wmc (classified by the `c t` header, layout-independent).

families.title: Model Counting Competition 2024 — tracks 1 (mc) + 2 (wmc)

reference.note: As mcc-2023. Track 2b (negative/zero weights) is OUT OF SCOPE for our nonnegative-rational semiring and is skipped.

families.notes: 415.8 MB compressed but 60.3 GB uncompressed ACROSS ALL 5 TRACKS. fetch_mcc.py selectively extracts only mc+wmc members (never the full archive) to respect the 12 GB bank cap.

families.title: UAI inference competition — PR (partition function) corpus
families.conversion: uai_to_wcn.py (BINARY-domain instances only; multi-valued SKIPPED, not log-encoded — survey gap #2)

reference.note: PR ground truth is log10 Z in a companion *.PR / *.uai.PR solution file where present; our exact rational Z is compared as log10 within tolerance.

families.notes: Native-table home turf; zero encoding loss. Convertible subset = all-binary-cardinality instances (Grids/Ising subsets); multi-valued (linkage/Promedus/protein) skipped with a manifest note. Convertible/skipped tallies filled in at fetch time.

families.title: ADDMC 1,914-instance weighted suite (Cachet-Bayes + CRIL-Non-Bayes)
families.license: as-is (Cachet lineage; contains no OSI license)

reference.note: No per-instance reference counts published; ADDMC/DPMC papers report aggregates only. Verify by cross-checking small instances against sharpSAT-TD.

families.notes: Apples-to-apples with every DP-side baseline's published numbers (ADDMC/DPMC/TensorOrder2 all evaluate on this exact set). The Rochester source URL is dead; this v1.0.0 release asset is live.

families.title: Model Counting in the Wild (KR 2024) — model-counting archive (incl. 411 crypto instances)
families.notes: The 411 chosen-ciphertext CRYPTO instances (Beck-Zinkus-Green, USENIX 2020) live inside model_counting_benchmarks.zip — the one citable crypto-counting family, closing the crypto omission. XOR-hard: a γ-fingerprint predicted-hard target.

families.title: Model Counting in the Wild (KR 2024) — projected archive (117 QIF + more)
families.format: DIMACS-CNF with `c p show` projection headers
families.notes: ARCHIVE-NOW while the link is alive; 1.4 GB. Needs projected counting (M3): our parser correctly REJECTS `c p show` today.

families.title: Klebanov QIF benchmarks (qif-cnf.tgz)
families.notes: ARCHIVE-NOW: the companion ApproxMC-p solver dir already 404'd; only this instance tarball survives. Deferred until M3.
